commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
Renames the agent system prompt timezone-only section from `Current Date & Time` to `Time Zone` and updates the matching unit-test assertion.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests 0. Total 0 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source renders `## Current Date & Time` with only a timezone line, and the linked report provides the user-facing temporal-reasoning failure mode; I did not need a live model run to prove the prompt-shape mismatch.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can update the stale docs references without changing the prompt behavior or adding timestamps.

target.title: fix(agents): rename misleading system prompt section to Time Zone
target.body:
## Problem

The system prompt section for user timezone information is titled `## Current Date & Time`, but it only contains the timezone string (e.g. `Time zone: America/Chicago`). The actual current date and time are intentionally omitted for prompt cache stability — this is documented and correct behavior.

However, the misleading title creates a contradiction that increases the chance of temporal reasoning errors by the model. When a user asks about a milestone being due "today" or "yesterday", the model may incorrectly infer dates because the section heading implies a full timestamp is available when it is not.

This was reported in #63181 with a concrete example: an agent described an April 8 milestone as "due yesterday" on April 8 itself, likely because the `## Current Date & Time` heading created a false affordance.

## Fix

Rename the section heading from `## Current Date & Time` to `## Time Zone` to accurately reflect the content and eliminate the misleading implication.

**Files changed:**
- `src/agents/system-prompt.ts` — rename heading in `buildTimeSection()`
- `src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts` — sync test assertion

This is a minimal, non-breaking change: the section still contains only the timezone, and the existing "use `session_status` for current date/time" guidance remains unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

### 1. Problem description
Before the fix, the system prompt contained a section titled `## Current Date & Time` that only provided the timezone:

```text
## Current Date & Time
Time zone: America/Chicago
```

This title misrepresents the content and has been shown to cause temporal reasoning errors (see #63181).

### 2. Test environment
- **OS**: Linux 6.8.0-71-generic
- **Node**: v24.14.1
- **Commit**: `HEAD` of `fix/misleading-prompt-section-63181`
- **Test command**: `npx vitest run src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`

### 3. Reproduction (before fix)

```bash
git checkout main
grep -n "Current Date & Time" src/agents/system-prompt.ts
# Line 390: return ["## Current Date & Time", `Time zone: ${params.userTimezone}`, ""];
```

### 4. After fix

```bash
git checkout fix/misleading-prompt-section-63181
grep -n "Time Zone" src/agents/system-prompt.ts
# Line 390: return ["## Time Zone", `Time zone: ${params.userTimezone}`, ""];
```

### 5. Regression test

The existing test `buildAgentSystemPrompt` → `timezone-only` case already validates that the prompt contains the heading. This test was updated to match the new heading:

```diff
- expect(prompt, testCase.name).toContain("## Current Date & Time");
+ expect(prompt, testCase.name).toContain("## Time Zone");
```

### 6. Test results

```bash
$ npx vitest run src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       15 passed (15)
Duration    2.45s
```

All `system-prompt` tests pass. The change is isolated to the heading string and does not affect prompt caching, section ordering, or any other behavior.

## Related
- Fixes #63181
- Related to #38782 (temporal grounding in startup greetings)
- Related to #63071 (prompt cache stability vs timestamp injection)
target.author: xuzehan-icenter

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
